description_long: This object is a vintage television with a large, convex glass screen set within a thick, cream-colored plastic housing. The front features a prominent, dark-tinted display window, surrounded by a wide, rounded bezel that gives it a retro aesthetic. The sides and back are solid and boxy, with ventilation slats and a power switch visible on one side, and several ports and controls recessed into the top panel. The bottom has mounting points and additional access panels, while the top includes rotary dials and colored buttons, likely for channel and volume control. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. This television would be typical of late 20th-century consumer electronics, often found in homes, garages, or public waiting areas.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a convex glass screen, thick cream plastic housing, and top-mounted controls. The design is utilitarian and retro, with visible vents and ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large, convex glass screen and thick, rounded cream bezel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying ventilation slats, a power switch, and the boxy profile.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing mounting points, access panels, and the recessed top control area.
description_view_3: Right side view with additional ventilation slats and a clean, unadorned surface.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, rounded cream plastic bezel with a smooth finish and retro styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and colored buttons on the top panel.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
